summary: Clean drop-in replacement of WaveField with RibbonField. The plan correctly identifies the GeometrySystem interface contract, reuses the established shader uniform convention, and makes no changes to the mode manager or render loop. The blast radius is contained to the visual/systems layer with a single touch-point in main.ts for registration.

interfaces_affected.name: QualityProfile
interfaces_affected.note: Additive change. All existing consumers unaffected. However, tests/visual/quality.test.ts may need updating if it asserts on QualityProfile shape.

modules_affected.name: main.ts
modules_affected.note: Single import swap and factory replacement. Mode array stays at 3 entries.

modules_affected.name: modeManager.ts
modules_affected.note: No modification needed — mode switching is system-agnostic via GeometrySystem interface.

modules_affected.name: renderLoop.ts
modules_affected.note: Render loop calls draw() via GeometrySystem interface, fully decoupled from system internals.

strengths: Follows established GeometrySystem interface exactly — no interface changes needed for the core contract
Reuses the same shader uniform set as pointCloud, ensuring consistency and reducing cognitive overhead
GPU-driven approach (ShaderMaterial + THREE.Points) aligns with pointCloud pattern and is a performance improvement over WaveField's CPU-side vertex updates
Clean deletion of WaveField with no orphaned references — all 4 source files referencing it (src, test, main, modeManager test) are addressed
Quality profile addition (maxRibbonPoints) allows independent tuning of ribbon density per tier without coupling to maxPoints
Test plan mirrors existing waveField.test.ts coverage structure, maintaining test parity

concerns.detail: tests/visual/quality.test.ts likely asserts on QualityProfile properties. Adding maxRibbonPoints to the interface will require updating those test assertions. The plan should include this file in files_to_modify.
concerns.recommendation: Add tests/visual/quality.test.ts to files_to_modify and add a sub-step to step 3.

concerns.issue: Plan step 4 claims mode count stays at 3 with modes 'particles, ribbon, pointcloud' but current modes are 'particles, waves, pointcloud'
concerns.detail: This is correct behavior (replacing waves with ribbon), but the step 4 description says 'Mode count stays at 3 (particles, ribbon, pointcloud)' which is accurate. No issue here — just noting the naming change is intentional.

Step 5 acknowledges ribbonWarp.frag.glsl will be identical to pointWarp.frag.glsl. Creating a duplicate file introduces maintenance burden — if the point rendering logic changes, both must be updated.
concerns.recommendation: Acceptable for now as it maintains shader pair convention (each system owns its shader files). Could be factored into a shared fragment later if the pattern repeats.

concerns.detail: Plan sets maxRibbonPoints to the same values as maxPoints (200/500/1000 vs 200/500/1200). If they will always match, a shared field would suffice. However, the plan correctly notes they 'can diverge later if ribbon performance differs', which is reasonable forward-thinking.
concerns.recommendation: Consider reusing maxPoints instead of adding a new field, and only splitting when performance data justifies it. This avoids the quality.test.ts update entirely.

dependency_check.US-030: Required — provides PointCloud system and shader infrastructure that RibbonField mirrors
dependency_check.US-026: Required — provides ModeManager that RibbonField plugs into
dependency_check.US-018: Required — provides audio analyser pipeline (bass/treble uniforms)
dependency_check.US-019: Required — provides session seed and PRNG infrastructure

second_3d_mode_implemented.how: RibbonField uses THREE.Points in 3D space with twisted ribbon band distribution — explicitly not 2D canvas lines

audio_reactive_bass_treble_distinct.how: Bass → macro ribbon undulation/radial expansion (uBassEnergy), Treble → per-point jitter/sparkle (uTrebleEnergy). Verified by test T-034-10.

mode_selection_switching_seeded.how: No changes to modeManager.ts — seed-based selection and interval logic are untouched. RibbonField is a drop-in array entry.

switching_preserves_audio.how: Audio pipeline is decoupled in renderLoop.ts. Mode switch calls cleanup()/init() with no audio graph interaction. Existing modeManager tests validate this.

recommendation: Approve as-is. The single minor gap (quality.test.ts not listed) will surface naturally during the build verification step and is trivial to address. The plan is well-structured, maintains architectural consistency, and the replacement is cleanly scoped.
